task_name: None - Instructional DIY FLS Training Setup
notes: This video is purely instructional/educational content showing how to build a DIY laparoscopic surgery practice trainer at home. It contains narrated step-by-step instructions, product demonstrations, and setup guidance. No actual FLS task performance is being executed.
content_summary: Video demonstrates construction of a homemade laparoscopic skills trainer using cardboard box, wooden spoons, rope/gino rossi material, basic tools, and a smartphone camera. Includes setup for orientation/depth perception practice, eye-hand coordination exercises, and peg transfer simulation using small objects.

demonstrated_techniques: Creating a laparoscopic trainer box from cardboard
Installing camera mount for video feedback
Setting up webcam using smartphone flash and free apps (DroidCam, IP WebCam, EpocCam)
Creating port holes for instrument access
Designing orientation/depth perception exercises with paper holes
Creating objects for eye-hand coordination practice (beans, small figurines)
Page-turning exercise for fine motor control
verbal_cues: Practice basic laparoscopic skills at home
Choose hole placement 3-5cm from side, 10-14cm from each other
Avoid touching trainer walls while passing instruments
Use smartphone flash as light source
Make holes slightly larger than instrument diameter for safety
common_errors_addressed: Inappropriate hole sizing (too small causes binding, too large loses realism)
Poor camera positioning limiting field of view

scoring_result: NOT SCORED — Video contains no actual FLS task performance. This is purely educational/instructional content showing DIY trainer construction and general laparoscopic skill practice concepts.
reason_not_scored: No performance video of any official FLS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture) is present. Content is demonstrative only, with narration and equipment setup focus.
